TC1.rationale: All claims are grounded and verified. No scale or period mismatches. All recomputations and P&L checks successful. Coverage meets threshold.

TC2.rationale: Issues detected: low coverage (0.0%), many unsupported claims (1). Requires review.

TC3.rationale: All claims are grounded and verified. No scale or period mismatches. All recomputations and P&L checks successful. Coverage meets threshold.

TC4.rationale: Issues detected: low coverage (0.0%), many unsupported claims (1). Requires review.

tc3_explanation: TC3 has scale_mismatch_count=0. The claim '$383 billion' is parsed and scale-expanded to 383,000,000,000. The evidence value (383,285 million) is also expanded to 383,285,000,000. Both values are stored in absolute form after unit normalisation, so the grounding engine compares them numerically: relative_error = 285M/383285M = 0.074%, within the 1% tolerance window. No scale_mismatch signal is raised because the units were successfully reconciled during normalisation. The scale integrity FLAG rule requires scale_mismatch_count>0, which is never set here. Catching this case requires a label-level check: detecting that the answer's scale_label='B' (billion) disagrees with the evidence's scale_label='M' (million) even when the numeric values agree after expansion. This is a separate enhancement, tracked as Prompt 3 of tolerance/scale-label design.
